title: Verification of NAD(P)H Concentration by NanoDrop
author: Daniel Moreira

steps_raw.instruction: Open the NanoDrop software and select UV-Vis or the custom absorbance measurement mode.
Set the measurement wavelength to 340 nm and the extinction coefficient to 6.22 mM −1 ·cm −1 if the software prompts for it.
Clean the upper and lower pedestals with a dry lint-free wipe.
Pipette 1–2 µL of blank solvent onto the lower pedestal and close the arm.
If the stock concentration is ≥ 1 mM, prepare a diluted working sample (e.g., 1:20–1:100 in blank solvent) before measuring.
Pipette 1–2 µL of sample (or diluted sample) onto the lower pedestal and close the arm.
Wipe both pedestals clean and repeat Step 6 for a total of 3 independent readings.
Register the mean ± SD of the three measurements.

Calculate the stock concentration using the formula: c_{\mathrm{stock}}\,(\mathrm{mM}) = \left(\frac{A_{340}}{6.22}\right)\times \text{dilution factor} If the sample was measured undiluted, the dilution factor is 1.

Example - A 340 = 0.245 for a sample diluted 1:100 from the stock c_{\mathrm{stock}}\,(\mathrm{mM}) = \left(\frac{0.245}{6.22}\right)\times 200 c_{\mathrm{stock}}\,(\mathrm{mM}) = 0.039\times 200 c_{\mathrm{stock}}\,(\mathrm{mM}) = 3.94

Record the following in your lab notebook: - Date of measurement - Nucleotide identity (NADH or NADPH) and preparation date - Solvent used for blank - Dilution factor applied before measurement - Three A 340 readings and calculated stock concentrations - Mean ± SD - Any deviations from this protocol
Record the A 340 and the calculated concentration.
